full_translation:
ANNOUNCER: 这里是CNN突发新闻！
AUDIE CORNISH, CNN THIS MORNING主播: 我们正在关注中东的突发新闻。以色列和伊朗互相发射导弹，脆弱的停火协议能否经受住这次考验。大家早上好，我是Audie Cornish。我们将从过去几小时发生的袭击开始。以色列称已识别出从伊朗发射的一波导弹。CNN连夜收到的新视频显示约旦河西岸一处房屋内部受损。此前，以色列袭击了伊朗一家石化厂。以色列国防军称击中了该综合体的多个目标。这些报复性袭击似乎无视了特朗普总统，他曾致电以色列总理内塔尼亚胡，要求他暂缓报复。随着中东紧张局势升级，油价也在上涨。布伦特原油和WTI原油今早均上涨。我们在该地区有现场团队报道。我们将从耶路撒冷的CNN记者Oren Liebermann开始。Oren，首先，你能告诉我们关于袭击的最新情况吗？你今天目睹了什么？
OREN LIEBERMANN, CNN耶路撒冷分社社长: 自当地时间晚上10点或11点左右以来，我们已经看到并听到了几波伊朗弹道导弹来袭。这已经持续了几个小时。第一波大约有三轮齐射。随后我们又看到几波后续的伊朗弹道导弹。据以色列军方称，其中大部分已被拦截。起初，他们说所有导弹都被拦截——但当我们跟进似乎显示导弹突破的伊朗视频后，他们表示这仅适用于较早的一波。因此，我们正在等待是否有更新。与此同时，伊朗军方称他们瞄准了以色列北部的拉马特大卫空军基地。以色列以自己的袭击作为回应，我们看到这些袭击从以色列驻美大使所描述的针对地对地导弹和非能源基础设施的袭击，逐步升级到你提到的对伊朗西部石化设施的打击。大约一个半小时前，以色列军方称他们打击了所谓的战略防御系统，这将为以色列空军打开天空，如果它朝那个方向行动的话。几个月前这场战争开始时，我们看到首批目标之一是伊朗的地对空导弹，其想法是，如果摧毁这些，以色列空军在很大程度上就能获得伊朗上空的行动自由。如果这变成联合行动，这也将包括美国空军。显然，这是我们正在密切关注的事情。关键是，这看起来正在升温，在升级阶梯上，也值得注意的是，Audie，也门的胡塞武装——伊朗的代理人——几小时前向以色列发射了至少一枚导弹，已被拦截。我们在耶路撒冷身后听到了拦截声。我们在全国其他几个地区也看到了来袭导弹的警告。在这里，以色列和伊朗都表示并公开声明，他们准备至少持续数天的这种状态。
CORNISH: 你谈到了升级阶梯，几周来我们一直听到以色列进入黎巴嫩南部追击真主党的行动。这里有黎巴嫩总统——我想播放这段视频，以便你帮助我们理解这些事件如何交织。这是他说的。
（视频片段开始）
JOSEPH AOUN, 黎巴嫩总统: 我们的人民正在被杀害，我们的房屋正在被摧毁。他们正在把黎巴嫩当作与美国谈判的筹码。这是不可接受的。
（视频片段结束）
CORNISH: 他当时是在谈论德黑兰。你能谈谈他们最近对黎巴嫩南部的袭击吗？为什么这引发了升级？
LIEBERMANN: 伊朗上周表示，如果以色列袭击贝鲁特首都，伊朗将直接攻击以色列。这正是我们在以色列和真主党之间非常动荡的周末所看到的。我们看到——根据黎巴嫩公共卫生部的数据，自这场战争大约三个月前开始以来，以色列在黎巴嫩的袭击造成的死亡人数已超过3600人。然后，即使在几天前美国斡旋的最新停火协议之后，我们看到真主党对以色列北部发动了袭击。这越过了以色列的红线，以色列随后对贝鲁特的达希耶社区发动了袭击，该社区被认为是真主党的据点。伊朗随后兑现了其威胁，于周日晚上直接向以色列发射了弹道导弹。这导致了我们现在的情况。总统约瑟夫·奥恩接受了我们的Christiane Amanpour采访，他明确表示他正在努力行使黎巴嫩主权，包括对伊朗代理人真主党进行某种控制，并试图找到某种协议，导致以色列军队从黎巴嫩南部撤出，使黎巴嫩更加完整。问题是，以色列政府现在既没有立场也不愿意从黎巴嫩南部撤军。伊朗政府将真主党视为其在黎巴嫩的立足点，真主党拒绝了最新的停火协议。这使得约瑟夫·奥恩总统处于非常困难的境地。他的黎巴嫩武装部队没有力量，也许也没有意愿对真主党采取任何严肃行动，尽管他试图取缔和禁止真主党的军事活动。关键的是，Audie，黎巴嫩发生的事情会影响美伊停火。伊朗知道这一点。他们知道唐纳德·特朗普总统想要达成协议，他们能够试图利用真主党，并在某种程度上利用黎巴嫩作为筹码，向特朗普施压，以在黎巴嫩问题上约束本雅明·内塔尼亚胡总理。
CORNISH: 有没有可能做到这一点？我想很多人都在看这些行动，并思考总统是如何与内塔尼亚胡交谈的，谈到降低温度。在以色列，人们如何看待特朗普的言论？
LIEBERMANN: 特朗普发表了一些公开声明，或者更确切地说，接受了一些记者采访，他说是特朗普在发号施令，而不是内塔尼亚胡，说他将敦促以色列不要报复或推迟以色列的报复。至少，这是公开信息的一部分。我们真正不知道的是，这也是我们更好地了解这里情况的关键问题，特朗普和内塔尼亚胡之间的电话内容是什么？有可能特朗普基本上开了绿灯，说以色列必须以某种方式回应，但将回应限制在一定范围内。也有可能特朗普告诉以色列不要回应，而内塔尼亚胡自行其是，尽管我认为这不太可能，因为内塔尼亚胡非常需要特朗普站在他一边，而且从未公开批评过他。所以，特朗普允许内塔尼亚胡做什么有一个范围。值得记住的是，特朗普曾迫使内塔尼亚胡结束了加沙、黎巴嫩以及之前的伊朗战争。我认为现在，人们仍然期望特朗普会再次这样做。问题在于何时以及如何。为此，特朗普需要基本上在华盛顿开始新的一天，这样我们才能了解更多幕后发生的事情。Audie。
CORNISH: 没错，我们在华盛顿醒来，Oren Liebermann从耶路撒冷与我们通话。我们的突发新闻报道在CNN THIS MORNING广告后继续。我们将讨论在什么情况下特朗普会考虑让美军回国？他的回答接下来。此外，菲律宾发生致命地震。紧急搜寻幸存者。
（商业广告）
[05:10:00]
CORNISH: 今早突发新闻。以色列和伊朗互相袭击，这是自4月停火协议以来最严重的升级。我们刚刚听到CNN的Oren Liebermann在耶路撒冷的报道，那里有警报声和拦截导弹后的爆炸声。一位美国官员告诉CNN，特朗普总统敦促以色列总理内塔尼亚胡暂缓对伊朗的报复。现在加入我们讨论军事战略的是退役空军上校、CNN军事分析师Cedric Leighton。早上好。首先，我想谈谈这次升级，因为人们可能几周来一直在听到以色列和真主党之间的来回交锋。为什么这一轮袭击会爆发？
CEDRIC LEIGHTON, CNN军事分析师: 是的，早上好，Audie。这一轮袭击爆发的原因是以色列袭击了贝鲁特的郊区达希耶。那里是真主党的主要总部。达希耶社区有很多真主党人员，恕我直言。伊朗基本上出面保卫那里的真主党实体的原因是，真主党基本上是伊朗在该地区的主要代理人，他们需要真主党不仅监视以色列，而且可能发动对以色列的袭击。所以，每当真主党遭到破坏，伊朗不仅将其视为对真主党的破坏，也视为对其自身力量的破坏，尽管它们完全是分开的。所以，这基本上就是正在发生的事情。伊朗人决定是时候回应以色列的袭击了，尤其是像对达希耶那样明目张胆的袭击，在他们看来。他们这样做的原因是，他们希望停火不仅覆盖伊朗，也覆盖黎巴嫩领土，以色列在那里对真主党进行了多次行动。
[05:15:00]
CORNISH: 你谈到了代理人，这也让我想到也门的胡塞武装，因为有报道说他们想阻止船只通过红海，这对我们这些一直听说伊朗阻止船只通过霍尔木兹海峡的人来说听起来很熟悉。这是否威胁到更广泛的冲突？我们是否看到了迹象？
LEIGHTON: 是的，这有可能扩大为至少更大的地区冲突。所以，在阿拉伯半岛的东侧，有霍尔木兹海峡，这是我们谈论了相当长时间的地区。在西侧，有所谓的曼德海峡，那是红海流入印度洋的非常狭窄的点。所以，这些是所谓的咽喉要道。每当这些被切断，就会影响全球航运、全球贸易。这意味着价格上涨，石油变得更加稀缺。这造成了我们现在在世界许多地方看到的经济问题。
CORNISH: 我们听到特朗普总统将美国的参与描述为预防而非升级。我想播放一段他在NBC的'Meet the Press'上的发言。这是在所有关于这次升级的消息之前。但这是它。
（视频片段开始）
UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: 如果你不特别称之为战争，你会考虑将一些驻扎在那里的部队——他们是因为'史诗之怒'行动而被派去的——
DONALD TRUMP, 美国总统: 你是指地面部队——
UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: 回家？不——
TRUMP: 你是说——
UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: 如果技术上不是战争，你会考虑送他们回家吗？
TRUMP: 我们不需要那么多部队。我认为你只是——就像在谨慎行事。
（视频片段结束）
CORNISH: 我们在看什么？这是一种胡萝卜加大棒吗？意思是美国在进行谈判，而以色列在施加军事压力？
LEIGHTON: 嗯，有可能。你知道，当你看到特朗普总统的言论时，它们——一方面，他在对冲他的赌注。他确实承认可能撤出一些在中东的部队。我认为他指的是那些额外派来的部队，除了已经驻扎在中东的部队。所以，他不想做的是完全撤出该地区，这实际上是伊朗的目标。所以，如果他完全撤出该地区，那将满足伊朗。但这不会保护阿拉伯海湾国家，如沙特阿拉伯、卡塔尔、阿联酋、科威特和巴林。所以，这成为一个关键点。他实际上不能撤军，如果他不想示弱的话。我认为这成为这里的关键点。
CORNISH: 还有一件事。我们知道这些袭击早在今年年初就开始了，是美国-以色列联合袭击，美国提供了大量军事支持和情报支持给以色列。那么，我们现在看到的这些袭击，没有美国的支持可能发生吗？
LEIGHTON: 有可能。我认为我们到目前为止看到对伊朗西部和中部地区的有限袭击的原因是，以色列有能力自行进行这些袭击。他们有自己的空中加油能力，尽管与美国能提供的相比有些有限。一般来说，如果袭击范围更广或连续几天重复，那可能表明美国有更大的参与。可能有一些美国参与，但目前这些袭击完全有可能在没有美国直接参与的情况下发生。
CORNISH: 好的，那是Cedric Leighton上校，谢谢你的见解。感谢你。CNN THIS MORNING广告后，我们的突发新闻报道继续。
（视频片段开始）
UNIDENTIFIED MALE: 把这事怪到以色列头上，有点可笑。是伊朗决定打破休战的。
（视频片段结束）
CORNISH: 特朗普总统敦促以色列不要报复。我们将进一步讨论。此外，特朗普的笼斗比赛就在几天后。它会在第一轮之前被击倒吗？有一项新的诉讼试图阻止它。
（商业广告）
[05:20:00]
CORNISH: 现在是整点过后23分钟。以下是今早的五条新闻。民主党参议院初选候选人Graham Platner在缅因州举行了一场活动，在初选前两天，面对热情欢呼的人群。他宣称自己只是来自缅因州沙利文的某个普通人。
（视频片段开始）
GRAHAM PLATNER, 参议院初选候选人: 在历史上的这样一个时刻，如果我们能证明一个来自普通世界的普通人，只是带着一个信息站出来，说我们都必须共同努力，抵制那些世代剥削和压迫我们的权力结构。如果我们证明这行得通，我们就能向这个国家展示前进的道路。
（视频片段结束）
CORNISH: Platner仍然有望赢得周二的初选，尽管《纽约时报》发表了一篇报道，称Platner的三位前女友描述了'动荡和有毒的关系'。其中一次提到了身体恐吓。Platner否认了这一点。由于纽约今晚将举办NBA总决赛和特朗普总统的活动，安全担忧已经加剧，此前六人在宾州车站的一起持刀伤人事件中受伤。官员称一名受害者伤势严重，其他人受轻伤。嫌疑人已被拘留。菲律宾发生7.8级致命地震。该地区的视频显示巨大破坏，建筑物倒塌，数人死亡。救援正在进行中。几个小时后，被指控押注抓捕尼古拉斯·马杜罗突袭行动的美军特种部队士兵将出庭。Gannon Ken Van Dyke定于在曼哈顿联邦法院举行状态听证会。检察官指控他在1月抓捕马杜罗的突袭之前，通过预测市场进行与委内瑞拉相关的交易，赚取了超过40万美元。本周末在特朗普总统生日当天举行的笼斗比赛面临被取消的危险。两名弗吉尼亚居民提起诉讼，要求停止在白宫举行的UFC比赛。诉讼称，建在白宫场地上的'爪子'未经国会批准或环境审查。它还指出，UFC正在销售超过一百万美元的VIP套餐。他们辩称，由于特朗普持有UFC股票，他将从比赛中获利。比赛定于本周六举行。我们的突发新闻报道接下来继续。CNN在德黑兰现场，今早对以色列发出新的警告，更多导弹飞来。此外，选举六天后，希望成为洛杉矶英雄的真人秀反派，Spencer Pratt的市长竞选是否会崩溃？
（商业广告）
[05:30:00]
ANNOUNCER: 这里是CNN突发新闻。
AUDIE CORNISH, CNN主播: 我们正在报道今早的突发新闻。中东紧张局势再次升级，伊朗和以色列互相发射导弹。伊朗国家媒体发布了这些图像，显示导弹射向以色列。这是几小时前。以色列国防军表示正在拦截袭击。今早的战斗代表了自4月停火生效以来最严重的升级。两国在周日早些时候开始交换导弹袭击，一直持续到周一。伊朗官员表示，新的袭击是对以色列持续打击黎巴嫩的回应。
（视频片段开始）
EBRAHIM ZOLFAQARI, 伊朗革命卫队发言人（通过翻译）: 犹太复国主义军队必须停止对黎巴嫩南部郊区的袭击。如果它扩大对该地区的袭击或回应伊朗的行动，它将面临更毁灭性和令人遗憾的打击，破坏性袭击将开始针对该政权及其支持者。
（视频片段结束）
CORNISH: CNN高级国际记者Fred Pleitgen也从德黑兰发回了这篇报道。我们想指出，CNN在伊朗经政府许可运营，但保持对我们报道的完全编辑控制。
（视频开始）
FREDERIK PLEITGEN, CNN高级国际记者: 在我们俯瞰伊朗首都德黑兰的屋顶位置上，我们听到了几声非常响亮的砰声，可能是爆炸声，然后还有一些较小的、相当连续的响声，可能是

vocabulary.en: a ceasefire that is easily broken or not stable
vocabulary.excerpt: the fragile ceasefire survive this test
vocabulary.example_cn: 脆弱的停火能否经受住这次考验

vocabulary.en: showing disobedience or opposition to something
vocabulary.excerpt: those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump
vocabulary.example_cn: 那些报复性袭击似乎无视了特朗普总统

vocabulary.excerpt: told him to hold off on striking back

vocabulary.en: a large number of bullets, shells, or questions directed at someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: approximately three barrages in that first wave
vocabulary.example_cn: 第一波大约有三轮齐射

vocabulary.en: to stop or catch something that is moving from one place to another
vocabulary.excerpt: most of those have been intercepted

vocabulary.cn: 升级阶梯（冲突逐步升级的步骤）
vocabulary.en: a series of steps or stages in which a conflict becomes more serious
a person or thing acting on behalf of another
vocabulary.example_cn: 也门的胡塞武装；伊朗在那里的代理人
vocabulary.excerpt: the Houthis in Yemen; an Iranian proxy there

vocabulary.en: something that can be used to gain an advantage in negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: using Lebanon as a bargaining chip
vocabulary.example_cn: 把黎巴嫩当作谈判筹码

vocabulary.en: a limit beyond which someone's actions are considered unacceptable
vocabulary.example_cn: 这越过了以色列的红线

vocabulary.en: a secure position from which further progress may be made
vocabulary.excerpt: Iranian government that sees Hezbollah as its foothold in Lebanon
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗政府将真主党视为其在黎巴嫩的立足点

vocabulary.en: to control or limit something that has become too powerful or active
vocabulary.excerpt: to try to rein in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu
vocabulary.example_cn: 试图约束本雅明·内塔尼亚胡总理

vocabulary.en: to be in charge or make the important decisions
vocabulary.excerpt: it is Trump who calls the shots

vocabulary.en: permission to proceed with a project or action
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump essentially gave this the green light
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普基本上开了绿灯

vocabulary.en: a narrow passage that restricts movement or flow
vocabulary.example_cn: 这些是所谓的咽喉要道

vocabulary.en: to reduce the risk of loss by making multiple bets or taking multiple positions
vocabulary.excerpt: he's kind of hedging his bets here
vocabulary.example_cn: 他在这里有点两面下注

vocabulary.en: a court hearing to check the progress of a case
vocabulary.excerpt: due for a status hearing in Manhattan federal court
vocabulary.example_cn: 定于在曼哈顿联邦法院举行状态听证会

sentences.en: Israel says it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran.
sentences.cn: 以色列称已识别出从伊朗发射的一波导弹。
sentences.structure: 主句（Israel says）+ 宾语从句（it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中过去分词短语'launched from Iran'作后置定语修饰'missiles'。

sentences.en: Now, those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump, who called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and had told him to hold off on striking back.
sentences.cn: 现在，那些报复性袭击似乎无视了特朗普总统，他曾致电以色列总理内塔尼亚胡，并告诉他暂缓报复。
sentences.structure: 主句（those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump）+ 非限制性定语从句（who called... and had told...）
sentences.analysis: 非限制性定语从句修饰'President Trump'，从句中并列谓语'called'和'had told'，'had told'表示过去的过去。

sentences.en: According to Israel's military, most of those have been intercepted.
sentences.cn: 据以色列军方称，其中大部分已被拦截。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（According to Israel's military）+ 主句（most of those have been intercepted）
sentences.analysis: 现在完成时的被动语态表示动作已完成。

sentences.en: We saw at the beginning of this war a few months ago that one of the first targets was Iran's surface-to-air missiles, and the idea there is that, if you take those out, then Israel's Air Force has to a large extent, freedom of the skies over Iran.
sentences.cn: 几个月前这场战争开始时，我们看到首批目标之一是伊朗的地对空导弹，其想法是，如果摧毁这些，以色列空军在很大程度上就能获得伊朗上空的行动自由。
sentences.structure: 主句（We saw）+ 宾语从句（that one of the first targets was...）+ 并列句（and the idea there is that...），其中包含条件状语从句（if you take those out）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中'that'引导；'the idea there is that'中'that'引导表语从句；条件状语从句'if you take those out'。

sentences.en: Crucially, this very much looks like it's heating up, and on the escalation ladder, it's also worth noting, Audie, that the Houthis in Yemen; an Iranian proxy there, fired at least, one missile towards Israel several hours ago that was intercepted here.
sentences.cn: 关键是，这看起来正在升温，在升级阶梯上，也值得注意的是，Audie，也门的胡塞武装——伊朗在那里的代理人——几小时前向以色列发射了至少一枚导弹，已被拦截。
sentences.structure: 主句（this very much looks like it's heating up）+ 并列句（it's also worth noting... that...），其中'that'引导主语从句
sentences.analysis: 'it's worth noting that...'是固定句型，'that'引导真正的主语；'an Iranian proxy there'是同位语修饰'the Houthis'。

sentences.en: Iran said last week that if Israel were to carry out an attack, the capital of Beirut, then Iran would directly attack Israel.
sentences.cn: 伊朗上周表示，如果以色列袭击贝鲁特首都，伊朗将直接攻击以色列。
sentences.structure: 主句（Iran said）+ 宾语从句（that if Israel were to... then Iran would...），其中包含条件状语从句（if Israel were to...）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中使用虚拟语气（were to... would...）表示对将来情况的假设。

sentences.en: The problem is, you have an Israeli government that isn't in a position and doesn't want to withdraw from southern Lebanon now.
sentences.cn: 问题是，以色列政府现在既没有立场也不愿意从黎巴嫩南部撤军。
sentences.structure: 主句（The problem is）+ 表语从句（you have an Israeli government that...），其中'that'引导定语从句修饰'government'
sentences.analysis: 表语从句中'that'引导定语从句，从句谓语并列'isn't in a position'和'doesn't want to withdraw'。

sentences.en: His Lebanese Armed Forces don't have the power and perhaps don't have the will to carry out any serious action against Hezbollah, even as he has tried to outlaw and ban Hezbollah's military activity.
sentences.cn: 他的黎巴嫩武装部队没有力量，也许也没有意愿对真主党采取任何严肃行动，尽管他试图取缔和禁止真主党的军事活动。
sentences.structure: 主句（His Lebanese Armed Forces don't have the power and perhaps don't have the will to carry out...）+ 让步状语从句（even as he has tried to outlaw and ban...）
sentences.analysis: 'even as'引导让步状语从句，表示'尽管'。

sentences.en: What's crucial here, Audie, is that what happens in Lebanon affects the U.S.-Iran ceasefire.
sentences.cn: 关键的是，Audie，黎巴嫩发生的事情会影响美伊停火。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What's crucial here）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that what happens in Lebanon affects...），其中'what happens in Lebanon'是主语从句
sentences.analysis: 多重从句嵌套：'What's crucial here'是主语从句，'that'引导表语从句，表语从句中'what happens in Lebanon'是主语从句。

sentences.en: They know that President Donald Trump wants to get to a deal, and they're able to try to use Hezbollah and to some extent, use Lebanon as a bargaining chip to pressure Trump to try to rein in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when it comes to Lebanon.
sentences.cn: 他们知道唐纳德·特朗普总统想要达成协议，他们能够试图利用真主党，并在某种程度上利用黎巴嫩作为筹码，向特朗普施压，以在黎巴嫩问题上约束本雅明·内塔尼亚胡总理。
sentences.structure: 主句（They know）+ 宾语从句（that President Donald Trump wants to get to a deal）+ 并列句（and they're able to try to use... to pressure...），其中包含不定式短语作目的状语
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句由'that'引导；并列句中'to pressure...'是不定式短语作目的状语；'when it comes to Lebanon'是时间状语从句。

sentences.en: It is possible that Trump essentially gave this the green light and said, yes, Israel has to respond in some fashion, but keep that response limited.
sentences.cn: 有可能特朗普基本上开了绿灯，说，是的，以色列必须以某种方式回应，但将回应限制在一定范围内。
sentences.structure: 主句（It is possible）+ 主语从句（that Trump essentially gave... and said...），其中'said'后接直接引语
sentences.analysis: 'It is possible that...'是固定句型，'that'引导主语从句；直接引语中'yes'是感叹词，'Israel has to respond... but keep...'是并列句。

sentences.en: It's also possible that Trump told Israel not to respond, and Netanyahu is acting on his own here, though I think that's unlikely, given how much Netanyahu needs Trump to be on his side, and has never sort of publicly criticized him.
sentences.cn: 也有可能特朗普告诉以色列不要回应，而内塔尼亚胡自行其是，尽管我认为这不太可能，因为内塔尼亚胡非常需要特朗普站在他一边，而且从未公开批评过他。
sentences.structure: 主句（It's also possible）+ 主语从句（that Trump told Israel not to respond, and Netanyahu is acting...）+ 让步状语从句（though I think that's unlikely）+ 原因状语（given how much...）
sentences.analysis: 'It's also possible that...'是固定句型；'though'引导让步状语从句；'given'是介词，表示'考虑到'，后接名词性从句'how much...'。

sentences.en: The reason this particular round of strikes kicked off was because the Israelis hit the suburb of Beirut, suburb of Dahiya.
sentences.cn: 这一轮袭击爆发的原因是以色列袭击了贝鲁特的郊区达希耶。
sentences.structure: 主句（The reason... was because...），其中'this particular round of strikes kicked off'是定语从句修饰'reason'
sentences.analysis: 'The reason... was because...'是常见结构，但更规范的说法是'The reason... is that...'；这里'because'引导表语从句。

sentences.en: Every time that there is destruction to Hezbollah, Iran sees this as not only destruction of Hezbollah, but also of their own forces, even though they are absolutely separate.
sentences.cn: 每当真主党遭到破坏，伊朗不仅将其视为对真主党的破坏，也视为对其自身力量的破坏，尽管它们完全是分开的。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句（Every time that there is destruction...）+ 主句（Iran sees this as not only... but also...）+ 让步状语从句（even though they are absolutely separate）
sentences.analysis: 'Every time that'引导时间状语从句；'not only... but also...'连接并列宾语；'even though'引导让步状语从句。

sentences.en: And the reason they did that is because they want the ceasefire to cover not just Iran, but also the Lebanese territory, where the Israelis have conducted repeated actions against Hezbollah.
sentences.cn: 他们这样做的原因是，他们希望停火不仅覆盖伊朗，也覆盖黎巴嫩领土，以色列在那里对真主党进行了多次行动。
sentences.structure: 主句（The reason they did that is because...），其中'they did that'是定语从句修饰'reason'，'because'引导表语从句，表语从句中包含非限制性定语从句（where the Israelis have conducted...）
sentences.analysis: 'The reason... is because...'结构；'where'引导非限制性定语从句修饰'Lebanese territory'。

sentences.en: So, on the eastern side of the Arabian Peninsula, you have the Strait of Hormuz, which is the area that we've been talking about for quite some time.
sentences.cn: 所以，在阿拉伯半岛的东侧，有霍尔木兹海峡，这是我们谈论了相当长时间的地区。
sentences.structure: 地点状语（on the eastern side...）+ 主句（you have the Strait of Hormuz）+ 非限制性定语从句（which is the area that...），其中包含限制性定语从句（that we've been talking about）
sentences.analysis: 'which'引导非限制性定语从句修饰'Strait of Hormuz'；'that'引导限制性定语从句修饰'area'。

sentences.en: And then on the western side, you have what's known as the Bab al-Mandab, and that is that very narrow point between the Red Sea as it empties out into the Indian Ocean.
sentences.cn: 在西侧，有所谓的曼德海峡，那是红海流入印度洋的非常狭窄的点。
sentences.structure: 并列句（you have what's known as... and that is...），其中第一个分句包含宾语从句（what's known as...），第二个分句包含时间状语从句（as it empties out...）
sentences.analysis: 'what's known as'是名词性从句作宾语；'as'引导时间状语从句。

sentences.en: Generally-speaking, if the strikes were much more extensive or repeated over a series of days, then that would probably indicate a greater U.S. involvement.
sentences.cn: 一般来说，如果袭击范围更广或连续几天重复，那可能表明美国有更大的参与。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if the strikes were much more extensive or repeated...）+ 主句（then that would probably indicate...）
sentences.analysis: 虚拟语气，'if'从句用过去式（were, repeated），主句用'would'表示对将来情况的推测。

sentences.en: The suit argues that the claw, built on the White House grounds was not approved by Congress or an environmental review.
sentences.cn: 诉讼称，建在白宫场地上的'爪子'未经国会批准或环境审查。
sentences.structure: 主句（The suit argues）+ 宾语从句（that the claw... was not approved...），其中过去分词短语'built on the White House grounds'作后置定语修饰'the claw'
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句由'that'引导；过去分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: They argue that due to Trump's stock in UFC, he would profit off the fight.
sentences.cn: 他们辩称，由于特朗普持有UFC股票，他将从比赛中获利。
sentences.structure: 主句（They argue）+ 宾语从句（that due to... he would profit...）
sentences.analysis: 宾语从句中'due to'表示原因。

topics.title: 以色列与伊朗的冲突升级
topics.content: 2026年6月，以色列和伊朗之间爆发了自4月停火以来最严重的导弹袭击。以色列袭击了伊朗的石化设施和战略防御系统，伊朗则向以色列发射弹道导弹，目标包括拉马特大卫空军基地。此次升级源于以色列对黎巴嫩真主党据点的打击，伊朗视其为对其代理人的攻击。美国试图通过外交手段降温，但局势持续紧张。
topics.keywords: Israel · Iran · missile attacks · ceasefire · escalation

raw_transcript:
CNN.com - Transcripts var cnnSiteWideCurrDate = new Date(2013, 1, 12); window.CNNSTATICSECTION = ''; var CNNENV = "//www.cnn.com"; Transcript Providers Return to Transcripts main page CNN This Morning Israel, Iran Trade Missile Attacks After Iran Targets Israeli Ramat David Airbase; Iran Threatens to Block Critical Bab al-Mandab Strait; Deadly 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Southern Philippines. Aired 5-5:30a ET Aired June 08, 2026 - 05:00 ET THIS IS A RUSH TRANSCRIPT. THIS COPY MAY NOT BE IN ITS FINAL FORM AND MAY BE UPDATED. [05:00:00] ANNOUNCER: This is CNN Breaking News! AUDIE CORNISH, ANCHOR, CNN THIS MORNING: We are following this breaking news out of the Middle East. Israel and Iran trading missile attacks, and the fragile ceasefire survive this test. Good morning, everybody, I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with the strikes that have happened in the last few hours. Israel says it has identified a wave of missiles launched from Iran. New video into CNN overnight showing damage inside a home in the West Bank. Now, this follows Israeli strikes on an Iranian petrochemical plant. The IDF saying that it hit several targets at that complex. Now, those retaliatory strikes appear to be in defiance of President Trump, who called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and had told him to hold off on striking back. As tensions rise in the Middle East, so do oil prices. Both Brent Crude and WTI Crude up this morning. We've got live team coverage throughout the region. And we're going to start in Jerusalem with CNN's Oren Liebermann. Oren, first, can you just give us the latest on the strikes, what you've witnessed today? OREN LIEBERMANN, CNN JERUSALEM BUREAU CHIEF: We have seen and heard several waves of incoming Iranian ballistic missiles since about 10:00 or 11 O'clock at night here local time. So, this has been going on for several hours. There were approximately three barrages in that first wave. And then we have seen a few subsequent waves of incoming Iranian ballistic missiles. According to Israel's military, most of those have been intercepted. At first, they said all of those -- but when we followed up after what appeared to be some Iranian video of missiles getting through, they said it only applied to an earlier wave. So, we're waiting to see if there is an update there. Meanwhile, Iran's military said they targeted the Ramat David Air Force Base in northern Israel. Israel has responded with its own attacks, and we have seen those progressively escalate from what Israel's ambassador to the U.S. described as attacks on surface-to-surface missiles and non-energy infrastructure to the strike. You mentioned on the petrochemical facility in western Iran. And then just an hour-and-a-half ago or so, the Israeli military said they carried out strikes against what they called strategic defense systems that would open up the skies to the Israeli Air Force if it moves in that direction. We saw at the beginning of this war a few months ago that one of the first targets was Iran's surface-to-air missiles, and the idea there is that, if you take those out, then Israel's Air Force has to a large extent, freedom of the skies over Iran. And that would also include the U.S. Air Force should this turn into a joint operation. Obviously, that's something we're watching very closely. Crucially, this very much looks like it's heating up, and on the escalation ladder, it's also worth noting, Audie, that the Houthis in Yemen; an Iranian proxy there, fired at least, one missile towards Israel several hours ago that was intercepted here. We have heard interceptions behind us over Jerusalem. We have seen warnings of incoming missiles in several other areas across the country. Here, as both Israel and Iran have indicated and publicly stated that they are prepared for at least days of this to continue. CORNISH: You talked about that escalation ladder, and we have been hearing for weeks about Israel's sort of move into southern Lebanon as it goes after Hezbollah. We have the President of Lebanon here -- I want to play this clip so you can help us understand how these things intersect. Here he is. (BEGIN VIDEO CLIP) JOSEPH AOUN, PRESIDENT, LEBANON: Our people being killed, our people being -- our houses being destroyed. They are using Lebanon as a chip -- bargaining chip in their negotiation with the United States. It's unacceptable. (END VIDEO CLIP) CORNISH: So, he was speaking there about Tehran. Can you talk about their most recent strike in southern Lebanon, why it triggered this escalation? LIEBERMANN: Well, Iran said last week that if Israel were to carry out an attack, the capital of Beirut, then Iran would directly attack Israel. And that's what we saw happen over what was a very volatile weekend between Israel and Hezbollah. We saw -- we saw the number of people killed in Israeli strikes in Lebanon, according to the Lebanese Ministry of Public Health, rise above 3,600 since this war began some three months ago. And then, even since the latest U.S.-brokered ceasefire which was just several days ago, we saw Hezbollah carry out an attack on northern Israel. [05:05:00] That crossed Israel's red line, and Israel then carried out a strike on the Dahiya neighborhood in Beirut, which is considered a Hezbollah stronghold. Iran then carried out its threat or carried out on its threat launching those ballistic missiles on Sunday night directly at Israel. And that has led us to where we are right now. President Joseph Aoun, who was interviewed by our Christiane Amanpour, he made clear that he is trying to exercise Lebanese sovereignty, and that includes getting some sort of control over Iran's proxy, Hezbollah, and trying to find some sort of agreement that will lead to a withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon and make Lebanon more whole. The problem is, you have an Israeli government that isn't in a position and doesn't want to withdraw from southern Lebanon now. You have an Iranian government that sees Hezbollah as its foothold in Lebanon, Hezbollah rejected the latest iteration of the -- of the ceasefire there. And that puts the President Joseph Aoun in a very difficult situation. His Lebanese Armed Forces don't have the power and perhaps don't have the will to carry out any serious action against Hezbollah, even as he has tried to outlaw and ban Hezbollah's military activity. What's crucial here, Audie, is that what happens in Lebanon affects the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Iran knows that. They know that President Donald Trump wants to get to a deal, and they're able to try to use Hezbollah and to some extent, use Lebanon as a bargaining chip to pressure Trump to try to rein in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when it comes to Lebanon. CORNISH: Is there any chance of doing that? I think a lot of people are looking at these actions and thinking about how the President has spoken to Netanyahu, talked about taking down the temperature. What's the view in Israel about how Trump has been talking? LIEBERMANN: Trump made a number of public statements, or rather, gave a number of interviews to reporters in which he said it is Trump who calls the shots, not Netanyahu, saying he would -- he would urge Israel not to retaliate or delay Israel's retaliation. That's at least, the public part of the messaging. What we don't really know, and this is the key question for us to have a much better sense of what's happening here is, what was the phone call like between Trump and Netanyahu? It is possible that Trump essentially gave this the green light and said, yes, Israel has to respond in some fashion, but keep that response limited. It's also possible that Trump told Israel not to respond, and Netanyahu is acting on his own here, though I think that's unlikely, given how much Netanyahu needs Trump to be on his side, and has never sort of publicly criticized him. So, there's a spectrum here of what Trump is allowing of Netanyahu. It's worth remembering here that Trump has forced Netanyahu to end wars in Gaza, Lebanon, and previously in Iran. And I think right now, it's still the expectation that Trump will do that once again. The question is when and how. For that, Trump needs to essentially get the day started in D.C., so, we can learn more about what's happening behind the scenes. Audie. CORNISH: That's right, we're waking up here in Washington, Oren Liebermann talking to us from Jerusalem. Our breaking news coverage continues after the break on CNN THIS MORNING. We're going to talk about under what circumstance would Trump consider bringing U.S. troops home? His answer next. Plus, a deadly quake hits in the Philippines. The urgent search for survivors. (COMMERCIAL BREAK) [05:10:00] CORNISH: Breaking news this morning. Israel and Iran trading strikes in the worst escalation since the ceasefire agreement in April. We just heard from our CNN's Oren Liebermann in Jerusalem, where there are sounds of sirens and explosions after the interception of missiles. A U.S. official told CNN that President Trump urged Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold off on retaliating against Iran. So, joining us to talk more about the military strategy here is retired Air Force Colonel and CNN military analyst Cedric Leighton. Good morning. So, first, I want to talk about this escalation because people may be hearing about the back-and-forth between Israel and Hezbollah for many weeks. Why did this particular round of strikes kick off? CEDRIC LEIGHTON, CNN MILITARY ANALYST: Yes, good morning, Audie. The reason this particular round of strikes kicked off was because the Israelis hit the suburb of Beirut, suburb of Dahiya. That's where Hezbollah has its main headquarters. And there are a lot of Hezbollah personnel for lack of a better term, in the Dahiya neighborhood. So, the reason the Iranians basically came to the defense of the Hezbollah entities there was because Hezbollah is basically Iran's main proxy in the region, and they need Hezbollah in order not only to keep an eye on Israel, but also to potentially mount attacks on Israel. So, every time that there is destruction to Hezbollah, Iran sees this as not only destruction of Hezbollah, but also of their own forces, even though they are absolutely separate. So, this is, in essence, what's going on. They decided -- the Iranians decided that it was time to respond to the Israeli attacks, and especially one as blatant as the one on Dahiya, in their view. And the reason they did that is because they want the ceasefire to cover not just Iran, but also the Lebanese territory, where the Israelis have conducted repeated actions against Hezbollah. [05:15:00] CORNISH: You talked about proxies, and then this also brings me to the Houthis in Yemen, because there's some reporting there that they were saying they wanted to prevent shipping through the Red Sea, which sounds very familiar to those of us who have been hearing about Iran preventing shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Does this threaten broader conflict? Are we seeing hints of that? LEIGHTON: Yes, there's definitely the possibility that this could widen into a bigger regional conflict, at least. So, on the eastern side of the Arabian Peninsula, you have the Strait of Hormuz, which is the area that we've been talking about for quite some time. And then on the western side, you have what's known as the Bab al- Mandab, and that is that very narrow point between the Red Sea as it empties out into the Indian Ocean. So, this is an area where these are so-called choke points. And every time those are cut off, that impacts global shipping, global trade. And it means that prices go up, oil becomes scarcer. And that creates the economic issues that we're seeing in a lot of the world right now. CORNISH: We hear President Trump framing the U.S. involvement more as precaution, not escalation. I want to play for you something that happened on "NBC's" "Meet the Press". This was before all of this news about this escalation. But here it is. (BEGIN VIDEO CLIP) UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: If you're not specifically calling it a war, would you consider sending some of the troops who are stationed there, who were sent because of Operation Epic Fury -- DONALD TRUMP, PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES: You mean boots on the ground -- UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: There at home? No -- TRUMP: Are you talking -- UNIDENTIFIED FEMALE: Would you consider sending them home if it's not technically a war? TRUMP: We don't need as many troops. I think you just -- like it's on the -- to err on the side of caution. (END VIDEO CLIP) CORNISH: What are we looking at? Is it a kind of carrot and stick? Meaning the U.S. is doing the negotiating, but Israel is leaning hard on the military pressure? LEIGHTON: Well, potentially so. You know, it's interesting when you look at President Trump's remarks, they -- you know, on the one hand, he's kind of hedging his bets here. He does, you know, admit to the possibility of withdrawing some of the troops that are in the Middle East. And I think what he's referring to, are the troops that came in, in addition to the ones that were already stationed in the Middle East. So, what he doesn't want to do is withdraw completely from the region, which is actually Iran's goal. So, if he withdrew completely from the region, that would satisfy Iran. But it wouldn't protect the Arab Gulf states like Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain. So, that becomes a critical point right here. He, in essence, really can't withdraw if he -- if he doesn't want to show weakness. And that becomes, I think, the critical point right here. CORNISH: One more thing. We know that these strikes started back earlier in the year. They were U.S.-Israeli joint strikes, the U.S. gives a lot of military support, Intelligence support to Israel. So, could what we're seeing right now even happen without U.S. support. LEIGHTON: It could. And the reason I think we see limited strikes in the western and central parts of Iran, at least so far, is because the Israelis do have a capability to conduct those strikes on their own. They have their own refueling capability, although it's somewhat limited compared to what the U.S. can offer. Generally-speaking, if the strikes were much more extensive or repeated over a series of days, then that would probably indicate a greater U.S. involvement. There may be some U.S. involvement, but it is absolutely possible that these strikes are occurring without direct U.S. involvement at the moment. CORNISH: OK, that's Colonel Cedric Leighton, thank you for your insight. Appreciate you. After the break on CNN THIS MORNING, our breaking news coverage continues.
